message:
fix(h2): cap HPACK encoder table size to 1 MiB + audit batch 387

Bug fix:
- h2/connection.rs: peer SETTINGS_HEADER_TABLE_SIZE could set the HPACK
  encoder table to u32::MAX (4 GB). Decoder was already capped at 1 MiB
  but encoder was not. Now both are capped symmetrically.

Deep security audits (all SOUND, batch 385-387):
- runtime/local.rs (163L) — 100% src/ file coverage achieved
- http/h1/server.rs + codec.rs — header injection, request smuggling, HEAD, Expect
- database/postgres.rs — SCRAM-SHA-256, SQL injection, TLS, wire protocol
- tls/connector.rs — cert verification, TLS versions, SNI, key material
- http/h2/connection.rs — stream IDs, flow control, GOAWAY, settings, priority
- service/load_balance.rs — in-flight tracking, SWRR, P2C, health checks
- scheduler/stealing.rs + local_queue.rs — work-stealing concurrency
- channel/mpsc.rs — two-phase reserve/send, waker dedup
- obligation/ledger.rs — leak detection, token validation, reset safety
- Diff audit HEAD~15 (10 files) — all SOUND

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
